subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from rulebook)
- **Base player range** — 3-6 players.
- **3 players** — Fully legal, but listed in game tips as highly tactical and not recommended for beginners.
- **4 players** — Uses normal base rules (same component distribution as 3-6).
- **5 players** — Uses normal base rules (same component distribution as 3-6).
- **6 players** — Uses normal base rules (same component distribution as 3-6).
- **Variant (2 boxes)** — 7-10 players by combining two copies of the game.
- **Variant (per player loadout)** — 2 tribes per player variant is listed.

#### Tribe Components (per tribe)
- **Flower discs** — 3 per tribe.
- **Skull discs** — 1 per tribe.
- **Playing mat** — 1 per tribe.

#### Total Base Box Counts
- **Tribes** — 6 total.
- **Total discs** — 24 total (18 flower + 6 skull).
- **Total mats** — 6 total.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
--- 3 Players ---
- **Tribe choice** — Each player chooses 1 tribe.
- **Disc count per player** — Take 4 discs (3 flowers + 1 skull).
- **Mat setup** — Place your mat skull-side visible.
- **Hand secrecy** — Keep discs hidden in hand.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Tribe choice** — Each player chooses 1 tribe.
- **Disc count per player** — Take 4 discs (3 flowers + 1 skull).
- **Mat setup** — Place your mat skull-side visible.
- **Hand secrecy** — Keep discs hidden in hand.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Tribe choice** — Each player chooses 1 tribe.
- **Disc count per player** — Take 4 discs (3 flowers + 1 skull).
- **Mat setup** — Place your mat skull-side visible.
- **Hand secrecy** — Keep discs hidden in hand.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Tribe choice** — Each player chooses 1 tribe.
- **Disc count per player** — Take 4 discs (3 flowers + 1 skull).
- **Mat setup** — Place your mat skull-side visible.
- **Hand secrecy** — Keep discs hidden in hand.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Round 1 Start
- **First player** — Choose a first player by any agreed method.
- **Immediate entry** — Start at Step 1 (Turn Preparation).

#### Step 1 (Turn Preparation)
1. **Choose disc** — Each player selects 1 disc from hand.
2. **Place face down** — Each player places that disc on their own mat.
3. **Advance phase** — Move to Step 2 (Adding Discs or Challenge).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Sequence
1. **Turn Preparation** — Every player places 1 face-down disc on their own mat.
2. **Adding or Challenge** — Starting with first player and moving clockwise, players add discs or start/raise bidding.
3. **The Attempt** — Final bidder (Challenger) flips discs equal to bid while following reveal-order rules.

#### Round Reset
- **After success/failure** — Resolve consequences immediately.
- **Next round lead** — Challenger is first player of next round, except where failure exceptions change lead player.
- **Repeat loop** — Return to Step 1 for the next round.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Step 2 Option A: Add a Disc
- **Add action** — Place 1 face-down disc on top of your own stack.
- **Visibility rule** — Offset discs so stack height is countable.
- **No-disc condition** — If you have no discs in hand, you must challenge.

#### Step 2 Option B: Challenge / Bid
- **Bid declaration** — Announce how many total discs you will reveal.
- **Minimum bid** — Cannot challenge for 0.
- **Maximum bid** — Cannot exceed total discs currently on all mats.
- **Bidding flow** — Clockwise, each player either raises the bid or passes.
- **Pass action** — Slide mat toward center to show you are out of bidding.
- **Raise amount** — You may increase by more than +1.
- **Direct jump** — You may bid straight to the maximum legal number.
- **Challenger identity** — Last remaining bidder becomes the Challenger.
- **Scope rule** — Challenge includes discs on all mats, including passed players’ mats.

#### Step 3: The Attempt (Reveal Rules)
1. **Own stack first** — Challenger must reveal all of their own discs first.
2. **Opponent choice** — After own discs, Challenger chooses opponents’ stacks in any order.
3. **Top-only reveal** — Reveal from the top disc of a chosen stack, one disc at a time.
4. **Partial stack allowed** — Challenger does not need to clear an opponent stack unless required by bid count.
5. **All-discs bid case** — If bid equals total table discs, still reveal one-by-one and stop at first skull.

#### Outcome Check
- **Any skull revealed** — Attempt fails immediately.
- **Bid count reached with no skull** — Attempt succeeds.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Failed Attempt Resolution
- **Immediate stop** — Do not reveal additional discs once a skull appears.
- **Disc return** — All players take their discs back into hand.
- **Penalty target** — Challenger loses 1 disc permanently.
- **Penalty method** — Challenger shuffles remaining discs face down.
- **Chooser rule (other skull)** — Owner of the revealed skull chooses which challenger disc is removed, unrevealed.
- **Chooser rule (own skull)** — If challenger revealed own skull, challenger chooses which disc to lose.
- **Elimination threshold** — Losing your last disc eliminates you.

#### First-Player Transfer on Failure
- **Other player’s skull flipped** — Next first player is that skull’s owner.
- **Own skull flipped** — Challenger chooses next first player.

#### Success Tracking
- **First success** — Flip your mat to flower side up.
- **Second success** — If already flower side up, you win immediately.

#### Special One-Disc Case
- **Adding phase** — A one-disc player must issue a challenge (cannot add).
- **Bidding rights** — A one-disc player may still pass or raise during bidding.
- **Elimination risk** — If that disc is revealed as a skull, that player is eliminated.

#### Official Variants Listed
--- 7-10 Players (Variant) ---
- **Table size change** — Combine 2 boxes for 7-10 players.

#### Official Variants Listed (continued)
- **Two-tribes variant** — Play with 2 tribes per player (as listed in rulebook tips).

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Win Conditions
- **Condition 1** — Succeed at 2 challenges.
- **Condition 2** — Be the only player not eliminated.

#### Scoring Model
- **VP system** — None.
- **Point totals** — Not used.
- **Tiebreakers** — None listed because victory is immediate-condition based.

#### Resolution Timing
- **Immediate end** — Game ends as soon as a win condition is met during normal resolution order.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early-Round Priorities
- **Count tracking** — Track exact disc totals on each mat from round 1.
- **Information bids** — Make early bids that test opponents’ confidence instead of only max-risk pushes.
- **First-player pressure** — If leading, force difficult bid decisions before stacks become predictable.
- **Pattern mixing** — Alternate flower/skull timing so opponents cannot map your rhythm.
- **Self-stack discipline** — Before bidding high, confirm your own top sequence is survivable because you must reveal it first.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Tactical density** — Expect tighter reads and faster punishment for predictable play.
- **Beginner warning** — Rulebook tip flags 3-player as less beginner-friendly.

--- 4-6 Players ---
- **Signal noise** — In 4-6 player tables, use table talk and timing shifts to create uncertainty among more observers.
- **Stack spread** — Exploit the larger number of opponent stacks when choosing reveal order after clearing your own.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Self-reveal oversight** — Forgetting you must flip all your own discs first leads to avoidable failed challenges.
- **Bad bid math** — Losing track of total discs creates illegal or reckless bids.
- **Single-target tunnel vision** — Revealing too deeply from one opponent stack ignores safer alternatives.
- **Predictable sequencing** — Repeating the same flower/skull timing pattern makes your stack readable.
- **One-disc panic** — Over-bluffing while on one disc often accelerates elimination instead of recovery.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### High-Impact Choices
- **Add vs challenge** — Add to increase ambiguity, or challenge now before opponents stabilize with safer stacks.
- **Bid ceiling** — Choose conservative survival bids versus aggressive two-win tempo bids.
- **Reveal routing** — After your own stack, pick opponent reveal order by behavior reads, stack depth, and current pressure state.

metadata.notes: Primary source: provided Skull official rulebook text summary (setup, three-step round flow, challenge rules, failure handling, elimination, victory, and listed variants).
